TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Bunun, story: bnNr-lazy-daughter_Hanaivaz

S.FORM: mihumisang na tahu saikin mas itu habasan mailantangus tu palihabasan.
S.TRANSL: 大家好，我要說的是很久以前的祖先的傳說故事。
Hello, I want to talk about a legendary story from long ago.

S.FORM: habas ang aiza dau pali’uni mas tupa tu aiza maz a makasa tu pinilumah masmuav tu malaitaz mais kuzakuza masmuav tu makasa at.
S.TRANSL: 很久以前有一個傳說說，有那個懶惰的媳婦，做甚麼工作動作非常慢。
A long time ago, there was a legend that said there was a very lazy daughter-in-law who did work slowly.

S.FORM: aupa aska'aun mas mabananaz hai musuhis isiadaan min‘uni mas hazam.
S.TRANSL: 因為被丈夫休妻回[娘]家在路上變成鳥。
She had been chased away by her husband and turned in a bird on the road home.

S.FORM: maupa cin tu.
S.TRANSL: 是這樣的。
That’s how it is.

S.FORM: aiza tu tas tu hanian aiza tacini maz a sia tu pinilumah.
S.TRANSL: 有一天 有一個媳婦。
One day, there was a daughter-in-law.

S.FORM: nii tu mahtu islunghu mais hanian maun mas haising.
S.TRANSL: 中午不能休息吃飯。
At midday they could not rest or eat.

S.FORM: ung at maz a tainilumah tu taini tas tu lumah pinilumah cia hai aupa maz a pinilumah a hai makasa at malaitaz at pilumahun saia mas kapinilumahan saicia.
S.TRANSL: 然後，屬於媳婦的那一家的主人，因為媳婦懶惰又動作很慢，他的婆婆公公把她放在家裡。
Then, as for the master of the family of the daughter-in-law, because the daughter-in-law was lazy and slow, her in-laws made her stay at home.

S.FORM: mudaan amin saicia mabananaz malavi amin mudaan kamaduh.
S.TRANSL: 他的丈夫也是這樣一起去要收小米。
Her husband also went along with all of them to harvest millet.

S.FORM: tas tu mintaunghu in a vali hai mikui nii ang aminun a tas tu maitasa tapaz a ma’ulan.
S.TRANSL: 正中午了，她連一把也沒剝完。
It already got to the noon but she hadn’t even finished stripping one string.

S.FORM: macimananu saia mababazu hai na mikui.
S.TRANSL: 她很用力一直稻米，但怎麼？
She pounded the rice very strongly, but how?

S.FORM: sanavan in hai nii ang lanahtung mabazu mas tas tu tapaz tu maduh tu.
S.TRANSL: 傍晚，她還是沒搗完一把串米。
By the evening, she still hadn’t finished pounding one string of rice.

S.FORM: pinunastu mas cina tu bazuav.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽辦下來的米飯。
The rice that mother had taken down.

S.FORM: at minsum in a maz a kaimaduh a bunun.
S.TRANSL: 可是那些收小米的人就回家了。
But then the people who were harvesting came home.

S.FORM: maina’iav tu bunun minsuma amin tu na kulumah in maun.
S.TRANSL: 那些幫助的人都回家來吃飯。
All of the people helping farming came home to eat.

S.FORM: at kulumah in a cina.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽進到家裡了。
And the mother came home.

S.FORM: minsum in a kaimin a kaimaduh.
S.TRANSL: 我們收小米的人回來了。
We the harvesters have come back.

S.FORM: masanmasauhdang amin a kaimaduh a bunun.
S.TRANSL: 全部收小米的人都很餓了。
We are all very hungry.

S.FORM: aupa habas Bunun mais kamaduh hai masamu islunghu at masamu maun.
S.TRANSL: 因為以前的布農人收小米的時候，不能休息吃飯 。
Because the Bunun people from the past, when harvesting millet were not allowed to rest or eat.

S.FORM: asa tu ka kanahtung mas mapandailaz tu manatpu tu kamaduhan at aupa kulumah maun hai.
S.TRANSL: 一定要先完成收整個行列的小米，才可以回家吃飯。
You need to finish harvesting the millet of a the whole row, before you can co home and eat.

S.FORM: uka pailu’ian a maz a ngul’a sia tu na pit’iaan.
S.TRANSL: 沒有鍋子在廚房。
There was no pot on the fire.

S.FORM: kudii sadu hai ai maz a nusung a ka malmiing a maduh nii ang aminun mabazu.
S.TRANSL: 她過去看，哎，臼裡面藏著未舂的小米！
She went over and saw, ai, in the mortar the unpounded millet was hidden.

S.FORM: singunguh masabah.
S.TRANSL: 呼呼地睡！
Snoring while sleeping!

S.FORM: ka nii tu haiaphaiap tu na mapit’ia.
S.TRANSL: 可是不知道要煮飯咯！
But doesn’t even know how to cook!

S.FORM: masubnuh maz a cina.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽非常的生氣。
The mother was very angry.

S.FORM: masubnuh amin a tama amin a sia s maina'iav tu bunun.
S.TRANSL: 父親、幫忙的人，大家都很生氣。
The father, all the people who came to help, all were very angry.

S.FORM: masubnuh daingaz a cina.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽非常的生氣。
The mother was very angry.

S.FORM: na ka’aun ku kasu.
S.TRANSL: 我不要你了！
I don’t want you anymore!

S.FORM: aupa tupaun a maz a tainisaicia mabananaz tu.
S.TRANSL: 然後她對丈夫說：
Then she tells the husband:

S.FORM: avunav isu a maluspingaz a.
S.TRANSL: “趕走你的太太！
“Chase off your wife!

S.FORM: tas tu hanian kaz dusa tu tapaz a maz a maduh a isbabazu at na mapakaun mas kaimaduh tu bunun.
S.TRANSL: 那天她只搗出來兩串搗米給農小米的人來吃。
That day she only made two strings of millet pulp for the millet harvesters to eat.

S.FORM: ka nii tu maipit’ia nii tu kanahtung mabazu.
S.TRANSL: 但她沒有煮菜，沒有搗完。
But she didn’t cook, didn’t finish pounding.

S.FORM: tuza tu maz a sia pinilumah a hai avunun naicia mapudaan.
S.TRANSL: 媳婦真的被他們趕走。
The daughter-in-law was chased away by them.

S.FORM: tudii tu labian avunun naicia mapudaan.
S.TRANSL: 那天晚上，他們趕走她。
Then that night, she was driven away.

S.FORM: mapising musuhis kusia ulumahun.
S.TRANSL: 不敢回到娘家。
I don’t there going back home.

S.FORM: habas mais nautu izaun in hai.
S.TRANSL: 過去以前假如被娶，
In the past, if you were married,

S.FORM: nii in mahtu minmazavmazav musuhis kusia ulumahun mais.
S.TRANSL: 沒辦法無緣無故回娘家。
you couldn’t go back to one's elderly home without reason.

S.FORM: nii tu ka patantungu mas madaingaz.
S.TRANSL: 除非是父母親叫你回去。
Except for when your parents would call you to come home.

S.FORM: at maz a pinilumah a hai makuang is’aang maz’av tu na mikua kata kulumah ka’aun.
S.TRANSL: 那個媳婦心理難過，害羞怎麼可以回娘家，我是被休妻的？
The daughter-in-law was very sad, too ashamed to go home, because she was chased off?

S.FORM: at masa na mudaan saia hai kikiva saia macistub mas mainangsa tu maduh cia.
S.TRANSL: 當她要走的時候，她最後一次切斷小米的莖
When she was about to leave, she cut millet stems for the last time.

S.FORM: luhusun saicia isaicia maz a bantas a bikni a.
S.TRANSL: 她把他的小腿綁起來。
She bound them around her lower legs.

S.FORM: aupa ka makuis tu maluspingaz tu ka uka s las at.
S.TRANSL: 因為那個女生很瘦，沒有肉。
Because she was very skinny, and didn’t have any meat.

S.FORM: at aupa sauamun mas ulalai.
S.TRANSL: 把這些綁腿巾綁起來。
She bound them into a shin pads.

S.FORM: iskamishang saia islabianan.
S.TRANSL: 她進入深夜了。
She went into the deep night.

S.FORM: at ka isia daan saia ai dikut sia daan saukutun istus’a hai.
S.TRANSL: 她在路上過夜一直到天亮。
She was on the road until dawn broke.

S.FORM: mudaan a saia avunun zami kui nii tu mahtu iskusia.
S.TRANSL: 她走了，我們把她趕走了，他沒辦法使用。
She went away, we chased her off, because she was of no use.

S.FORM: ilumah cin hai nii tu mahtu kuzakuza at.
S.TRANSL: 她在這個家，不能工作。
She was at home, because she can’t work.

S.FORM: tupaun zami tu musuhisa kusia isu tu ulumahun.
S.TRANSL: 我們叫她回娘家。
We asked her to go home.

S.FORM: mikui uka saulumah.
S.TRANSL: “可是沒有到家啊。”
“But she didn’t get back home!”

S.FORM: uka saulumah a maz a isaicia uvaaz a pingaz nauaz a.
S.TRANSL: 她女孩子沒有到娘家。
Her daughter did not make it back home.

S.FORM: lanasia itu cina tu tanangaus.
S.TRANSL: 落到媽媽的眼前。
And they fell in front of the mother’s feet.

S.FORM: saduan naicia hai ai maupa tu itu pingaznauaz tu ulalai.
S.TRANSL: 當他們看的時候，哎好像，是屬於女兒的綁腿巾。
When they took a better look at them, oh, they resembled the daughter’s shin pads a lot.

S.FORM: aupa masa mais mapaizaun a maz a uvaaz hai.
S.TRANSL: 因為當時假如小孩子被娶的時候，
Because when she was getting married,

S.FORM: mamantuk tu maz a cina hai kaupa s ka maitahis mas ulalai.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽一定有縫過她的綁腿巾。
The mother had made these shin pads for her.

S.FORM: ulus Bunun tu tasa mapa’adas mas pinanauaz cia.
S.TRANSL: 布農族傳統服裝給她帶的。
and had given her the traditional Bunun clothes to wear.

S.FORM: unghai sadua s cina cia muhalhal a mai ulalai.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽看到掉下來的是她之前縫過的。
The mother saw what came falling down had been sewn by her before.

S.FORM: at izaun izau s naicia cina cia masivit tuza tu inak tu ulalai.
S.TRANSL: 他們拿起來了，媽媽觀察，哎呀，真的是我的綁腿巾。
They picked it up, the mother studied and, oh my god, they were here shin pads.

S.FORM: mavia tu maupa cin tu isa bi s sia dau.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼這樣，她到底在哪裡了？
Why is like this, where is she?

S.FORM: isaincia saia ludun cia malala’la tu isa bi s kasu ni? isa bi s kasu uvaaz.
S.TRANSL: 她在山頂一直叫，你在哪裡了？你在哪裡了？
She was on the top of the mountain, and kept on calling: where are you? where are you?

S.FORM: isaincia cina a maupa cia tatanghis malala’la mas uvaaz cia hai.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽在那邊這樣的一直哭，一直叫孩子。
The mother kept calling like that for her child.

S.FORM: cisha aiza idadaza sia lukis ma’iang tupa tu “kaup in is’aang” “kaup in is’aang”.
S.TRANSL: 突然樹上有叫聲說：“只剩一口氣，只剩一口氣。”
All of a sudden there was a voice coming from a tree: “Only a whisper remains, only a whisper remains.”

S.FORM: malala’la maupa cia ta’azaun mas cina cia.
S.TRANSL: 有這樣的聲音被媽媽聽到的。
That’s what the mother heard.

S.FORM: at cinmangha maz a cina sadu tu maupa tu cina itu mai uvaaz tu sing’av a malala’la a dau.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽抬頭看到，好像是媽媽的孩子的聲音一直在叫。
The mother looked up — it sounds as if it’s her child who’s calling.

S.FORM: saikin hai isu tu cina.
S.TRANSL: 我是你的媽媽。
I am your mother.

S.FORM: na ‘adasun ku as kulumah hai.
S.TRANSL: 我要帶你會家。
I will take you home.

S.FORM: at samangha cina a sadu hai aiza tauk tasa hazam idadaza sia lukis cia.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽抬頭看到樹上有一隻鳥。
The mother lifted her head, and saw a bird in the trees.

S.FORM: malala’la kaup in tup in tu “kaup i s is’aang”.
S.TRANSL: 一直叫，只剩下說，只剩下一口氣。
It kept on calling, “only a whisper, only a whisper remains”.

S.FORM: amus tupahang dau sia uvaaz a tu uk in nak a is'aang mahtu saulumah.
S.TRANSL: 原來她的意思要說，我已經沒有力氣，可以回家了。
It turns out she meant that she didn’t have any strength left to go home.

S.FORM: at kaup in inak a is'aang mas ka isia puthul tan ‘uh’uh tan.
S.TRANSL: 我的氣只剩下在喉嚨的音。
Only a whisp of my strength remains.

S.FORM: samangha cina a sadu hai ai ka min’uni i s hazam idadaza sia lukis malala’la tu kaup i s is’aang.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽抬頭看到，她就變成鳥，在樹上面一直叫，只剩下喉嚨的一口氣。
The mother looked up and saw that she had become a bird, and kept on calling in the tree, only a whisp remained.

S.FORM: a saia tu hazam hai min’uni mas hazam hai.
S.TRANSL: 那隻鳥變成了鳥之後，
After the bird [she] became a bird,

S.FORM: cina hai makuang is'aang mudaan kulumah hai tu.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽難過回到家裡，可是??
the mother was sad and went home, but…

S.FORM: aiza saia tu hazam isia ludun.
S.TRANSL: 有這隻鳥在山上。
This kind of bird is still in the woods.

S.FORM: mais kusia ludun ispus hai uta’azaan mas maupa cia tu hazam malala’la tu .
S.TRANSL: 如果人去山上的森林的話，就會聽得到這樣的鳥叫聲，一直叫“lapus ‘ang’ang, lapus ‘ang’ang”。
If people go into the woods on the mountains, they will hear that kind of birdcall, “lapus ‘ang’ang, lapus ‘ang’ang."

S.FORM: a saia hai pacingaanan mas bunun tu tu hazam.
S.TRANSL: 牠從布農族收到“lapus ‘ang’ang”的鳥的命名。
The Bunun people gave it the name “Lapus ‘ang’ang” .

S.FORM: kaupa cia.
S.TRANSL: 結束了。
The end.
